display_name: A missing MPLS label usually means the prefix is missing from the IGP, not an LDP/SR problem
context: A prefix that should be label-switched has no label binding in the LFIB or LIB, or an LSP is incomplete
observation: No label exists for a prefix that should have one — 'show mpls forwarding-table' or 'show mpls ldp bindings' shows no entry for the destination
naive_interpretation: LDP or Segment Routing is misconfigured — the label distribution protocol is failing to allocate or advertise labels
correct_interpretation: LDP allocates labels for prefixes it learns from the IGP RIB. SR assigns labels based on the SID index from the IGP (IS-IS/OSPF). In both cases, if the prefix is not in the IGP routing table on the local router, no label is allocated or imported. The most common cause of a missing label is a missing IGP route — not an LDP/SR configuration error. Check the IGP first: is the prefix in the IS-IS/OSPF database and RIB? Is the advertising router reachable? Only after confirming the IGP route exists should you look at LDP session state, label allocation filters, or SR prefix-SID configuration.
why: LDP liberal-retention mode stores remote bindings, but the local label for forwarding is allocated only for prefixes in the local FIB/RIB from the IGP
SR prefix-SIDs are configured under the IGP (IS-IS/OSPF) and distributed via IGP advertisements — no IGP adjacency or route means no SID is learned
An IGP route withdrawal (due to metric change, passive-interface, redistribution filter, or area boundary) removes the prefix from the RIB, which cascades to label removal
LDP requires an LDP session AND a matching IGP route to create a usable label-switched path; either missing breaks the LSP

confirmation_commands.purpose: Verify the prefix exists in the RIB via IGP before investigating label allocation
Check if any local or remote label bindings exist for the prefix — absence confirms the IGP-first theory
Check the LFIB for the prefix label entry; no entry means no label was installed for forwarding
Confirm the prefix is in the IS-IS RIB; missing here means SR cannot allocate a label for it
Check label allocation and whether the prefix-SID for the destination is present in the label table

traps: Debugging LDP or SR configuration when the prefix is simply not in the IGP routing table
Restarting LDP sessions or clearing label tables when the root cause is an IGP adjacency or route-filter issue
Confusing LDP liberal-retention (stores remote bindings) with actual forwarding — remote bindings may exist but local label may be absent if the IGP route is missing
In SR environments, checking segment-routing config when the prefix-SID was correctly configured but the IGP route was withdrawn by the advertising router
